title: ROUGE-1, ROUGE-2, and ROUGE-L
concept: ROUGE-1 measures single-word overlap; ROUGE-2 measures bigram overlap; ROUGE-L measures the Longest Common Subsequence (LCS).

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of using the ROUGE metric in machine learning?

options.text: To grade AI-written summaries against human summaries
To speed up the internet connection of the AI
To translate English text into other languages

exercises.explain: ROUGE is a metric used to automatically evaluate and grade AI-generated summaries by comparing them directly to high-quality summaries written by humans.

cards.title: Grading the AI's Homework
cards.body: When an AI writes a summary of a news article, how do we know if it did a good job? We can't read millions of summaries ourselves! Instead, we use ROUGE to automatically grade the AI by comparing its words to a human's summary. Let's find out how it works!

cards.title: Meet the ROUGE Family
cards.body: ROUGE is a set of metrics used to evaluate AI-generated summaries by comparing them to human-written summaries. ROUGE-1 measures single-word overlap. ROUGE-2 looks at two-word pairs (bigrams). ROUGE-L tracks the Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) to check if words appear in a similar order.

cards.title: The Puzzle Magnifiers
cards.body: Imagine ROUGE as different magnifying glasses for looking at a puzzle. ROUGE-1 looks at single pieces (words). ROUGE-2 looks at pairs of pieces connected together. ROUGE-L looks at the longest unbroken line of pieces that match the picture on the box, even if there are some gaps in between!

cards.title: ROUGE in Action
cards.body:
Let's compare:
Human: "The dog barked"
AI: "A dog barked"

• ROUGE-1 looks at single words: "dog" and "barked" match (2 matches).
• ROUGE-2 looks at pairs: "dog barked" matches (1 match).
• ROUGE-L looks at the longest sequence: "dog barked" is the longest shared chain.

cards.title: Scrambled Words Trick
cards.body: You might think that if an AI gets a perfect ROUGE-1 score, its summary is perfect. Not true! An AI could write the words in a completely scrambled, meaningless order and still get a high ROUGE-1 score because it only checks single words. That is why we need ROUGE-2 and ROUGE-L to check word order!

cards.title: ROUGE-1 vs. ROUGE-2
cards.body: Let's compare how ROUGE-1 and ROUGE-2 view text matching. ROUGE-1 focuses on isolated words, while ROUGE-2 focuses on connected pairs.

right.text:
Counts pairs of matching words:
"fox jumps" = Match
"jumps over" = Match

left.text:
Counts single matching words:
"fox" = Match
"jumps" = Match

exercises.prompt: If you want to measure how many matching two-word pairs (like "red apple" or "sunny day") your AI summary shares with a human summary, which metric should you use?
exercises.explain: ROUGE-2 looks at pairs of consecutive words (bigrams). In "red apple", the only two-word pair is "red apple", which matches the human summary exactly!

exercises.prompt: True or False: ROUGE-1 measures the overlap of two-word pairs (bigrams) between the AI and human texts.
exercises.explain: ROUGE-1 only looks at individual words. It does not check if they are grouped into pairs; that is the job of ROUGE-2.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the explanation of ROUGE-L:
exercises.sentence: ROUGE-L is unique because it measures the longest matching sequence of words while preserving their original _______.
exercises.explain: ROUGE-L stands for Longest Common Subsequence. It is special because it measures the longest matching chain of words while keeping track of their relative order, even if there are extra words in between.

exercises.prompt:
Look at these two texts:
System A (Human): "The quick brown fox jumps."
System B (AI): "Fox quick brown the jumps."
Why is System B flagged as the AI-generated text despite having all the correct words?

options.text: System A is the AI because it has perfect grammar.
System B is the AI because the words are scrambled but match individual words.
Both systems are AI-generated because they have the exact same length.

exercises.explain: System B is the AI summary because it just lists the words from the human text in a random, scrambled order. It has high single-word overlap (ROUGE-1) but makes no sense. System A is the human-written reference text.

exercises.prompt: If you want to evaluate how well an AI kept the sentence structure and word order of a human summary like "The cat sat on the rug" vs "The cat sat on the mat", which ROUGE metric is best suited to find the longest matching chain of words in order?

options.text: ROUGE-1, because it only counts the word 'the' once.
ROUGE-2, because it looks at word pairs like 'cat sat' and 'rug mat'.
ROUGE-L, because it tracks the longest shared sequence of words in order.
None of them, because the last words of the sentences are different.

exercises.explain: ROUGE-L looks for the longest order-preserving chain of words (Longest Common Subsequence). 'The cat sat on the rug' and 'The cat sat on the mat' share the matching chain 'The cat sat on the', which is 5 words long!
